strengths: Demonstrated hands-on experience building event-driven systems and agentic workflows, including contributions to AI-agent infrastructure at re:cinq.
Strong backend and distributed systems background with production experience in high-throughput microservices, real-time data, and low-latency systems (e.g., at Dunnhumby).
Proven track record in engineering leadership, open-source project ownership, and technical mentorship through workshops, CI/CD setup, and team enablement.
concerns: No explicit evidence of leading or scaling an engineering team from 10 to 30+ people in a fintech or regulated AI context.
Lack of clear documentation of experience with financial systems, credit risk modeling, or compliance infrastructure critical to the role.
Resume uses inconsistent name (Aisha Okonkwo vs. Szabo Bogdan) and lacks quantifiable outcomes for AI-agent systems or fintech impact.
response.justification: The candidate brings strong technical depth in distributed systems, event-driven architecture, and early-stage agentic AI work, with evidence of craftsmanship and tooling contributions—but lacks demonstrable experience in regulated fintech domains, team scaling at the executive level, and clear alignment with credit-risk or working-capital systems required for the role.
